name: Tennis
description: Tennis is a single-player or two-player game simulating a tennis match. The game features two players positioned on opposite sides of a net, within a rectangular court. Players control their on-screen avatar to hit a ball back and forth, attempting to make it land within their opponent's court boundaries. A point is scored when a player fails to return the ball legally (i.e., hitting it out of bounds, failing to hit it before the second bounce, or committing a fault during service). The game proceeds with serving, returning, and volleying until one player accumulates enough points to win a game, set, and ultimately, the match. The ball should have realistic physics in terms of bounce and speed, and trajectory. The game should simulate basic tennis rules, including serving rules, scoring rules, and the concept of 'out' balls. A single-player mode should allow the user to play against an AI opponent of varying difficulty. The game requires accurate tracking of scores and game progression.

controls.arrow_up: Player 1 moves upwards within their court.
controls.arrow_down: Player 1 moves downwards within their court.
controls.w: Player 2 moves upwards within their court.
controls.s: Player 2 moves downwards within their court.
controls.arrow_left: Player 1 applies leftward spin to the ball during a hit.
controls.arrow_right: Player 1 applies rightward spin to the ball during a hit.
controls.a: Player 2 applies leftward spin to the ball during a hit.
controls.d: Player 2 applies rightward spin to the ball during a hit.
